spec: RFC 9449 (OAuth 2.0 Demonstrating Proof of Possession) + RFC 7638 (JWK Thumbprint)

tests.title: Generate a valid DPoP proof JWT
tests.given: A DPoP key pair (ES256 or RS256), the HTTP method POST, and the request URI https://server.example.com/token
tests.when: A DPoP proof JWT is generated for the request
tests.then: The compact JWS has a protected header with typ=dpop+jwt, alg equal to the key pair's asymmetric algorithm (never a symmetric algorithm such as HS256), and a jwk member containing ONLY the public key (no private key material such as d, p, q); and a payload with a non-empty unique jti, htm=POST, htu=https://server.example.com/token, and a recent iat timestamp

tests.title: Include the DPoP proof in the token request header without an ath claim
tests.given: A DPoP proof JWT generated for a token request
tests.then: The proof is carried in the DPoP HTTP request header (not in Authorization), and its payload contains no ath claim because token-request proofs do not bind to an access token

tests.title: Generate a resource-request proof carrying the ath claim
tests.given: A DPoP key pair and a DPoP-bound access token
tests.when: A DPoP proof JWT is generated for a protected-resource request
tests.then: The proof payload contains an ath claim whose value equals BASE64URL(SHA-256(access_token)) with no base64 padding, matching the deterministic access_token/ath pairs in dpop-ath-pairs.json

tests.title: Handle a use_dpop_nonce error and retry with the server nonce
tests.given: A token or resource request that receives HTTP 401 with error=use_dpop_nonce and a DPoP-Nonce response header
tests.when: The client automatically retries the request
tests.then: The retried DPoP proof includes the server-provided nonce in its nonce claim, and the nonce is cached and pre-included in subsequent proofs sent to the same server

tests.title: Verify cnf.jkt in a bound token matches the DPoP key thumbprint
tests.given: A DPoP-bound access token whose cnf claim carries a jkt field, and the DPoP key pair the token was bound to
tests.when: The RFC 7638 SHA-256 JWK thumbprint of the DPoP public key is computed
tests.then: The computed thumbprint equals the token's cnf.jkt value; each JWK in dpop-thumbprint-pairs.json (including the RFC 7638 §3.1 canonical RSA vector) reproduces its expected thumbprint

tests.title: Reject a DPoP proof with a wrong htm or htu
tests.given: A DPoP proof whose htm does not match the actual HTTP method, or whose htu does not match the actual request URI
tests.when: A resource server validates the proof against the expected method and URI
tests.then: The proof is rejected with a validation error that identifies the mismatched field (htm or htu); proofs signed with a symmetric algorithm or the none algorithm, or missing the embedded jwk, are likewise rejected

tests.title: Generate ES256 and RS256 key pairs
tests.given: A request to generate a DPoP key pair
tests.when: The algorithm is ES256, and separately when it is RS256
tests.then: ES256 yields a valid EC P-256 key pair and RS256 yields a valid RSA key pair with a modulus of at least 2048 bits; an unsupported algorithm is rejected with an error

tests.title: DPoP-bound token uses the Authorization: DPoP scheme
tests.then: The Authorization header uses the DPoP scheme (Authorization: DPoP <access_token>) rather than the Bearer scheme, and the accompanying DPoP proof header carries the ath claim
